nativeRange.habitat: The parent species, Microsorum pteropus, is native to Southeast Asian rainforest streams, riverbanks, and waterfall zones, where it grows epiphytically — anchored to submerged or emergent rocks and wood rather than rooted in soil. The Narrow Leaf form shares this origin and habit.

summary: The Narrow Leaf Java Fern is a cultivar of the standard Java Fern (Microsorum pteropus) with slimmer, more upright leaves and a finer texture. Care follows the same epiphytic rules as the parent species: the rhizome must never be buried, and the plant is attached to wood or rock rather than planted in substrate.
careNotes:
The Narrow Leaf Java Fern is a cultivar of Microsorum pteropus with leaves that are noticeably slimmer — around 0.5–1 cm wide — and grow at a less acute angle from the rhizome than the type form, producing a more upright, finer-textured plant that tends to work well as a midground accent on hardscape.

CRITICAL husbandry rule (shared with all Java Fern variants): the horizontal rhizome MUST stay above the substrate — burying it causes rot and plant death. Attach the plant to driftwood, porous rock, or other hardscape with thread, fishing line, or cyanoacrylate gel until the roots anchor naturally (typically several weeks). As an epiphyte (a plant that anchors to surfaces rather than rooting in soil), it absorbs nutrients primarily through its leaves and rhizome from the water column, so liquid fertilization is generally more useful than substrate dosing. CO2 supplementation is not required, though it modestly speeds growth.

Like the parent species, the Narrow Leaf form is generally not eaten by herbivorous fish, which makes it a good choice for goldfish and African cichlid setups that would shred softer plants. Small dark spots on the undersides of mature leaves are sporangia (reproductive structures), not disease.

Other Java Fern cultivars — Windelov, Trident, Needle Leaf, Philippine — have or will have separate entries; the standard type form is [fw-plant-019].
breedingNotes: The Narrow Leaf Java Fern propagates through the same two methods as the parent species. The first is rhizome division: a section of healthy rhizome bearing several leaves and roots is cut away and re-attached to new hardscape, where it will establish independently. The second is adventitious plantlets — small daughter plants that mature leaves spontaneously produce along their edges and tips. Once a plantlet has developed a few leaves and trailing roots, it can be detached and attached to its own piece of hardscape. Plantlet production has been reported to accelerate dramatically on stressed or dying leaves. True spore reproduction is possible from emersed fronds but rarely occurs under submerged aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary source for cultivar identity (Microsorum pteropus 'Narrow'), narrower leaves at a less acute angle from the rhizome, height (10–20+ cm after two months), rhizome length (10–15 cm or more), light demand (low), CO2 demand (low), growth rate (slow), placement (epiphyte, attaches to roots and stones), care difficulty (easy), and suitability for smaller aquariums.

additional.name: Aquatic Motiv
additional.notes: Used as the first secondary after the playbook-specified Buce Plant URL returned HTTP 404. Cross-checked native range (Malaysia, Thailand, India, China), temperature (67–84 °F ≈ 19–29 °C), pH (6.3–7.8), light (low), CO2 (not required), growth rate (slow), placement (epiphyte attached to rock/driftwood), care difficulty (very easy), tank size (~10 US gal floor), maximum height (~33 cm), and compatibility with African and South American cichlids.

additional.name: Bantam.earth (Vivarium Plants)
additional.notes: General Microsorum pteropus care guide referencing Narrow Leaf as one of the four common varieties. Cross-checked temperature (60–85 °F overall range), pH (optimal 6.0–7.5 with tolerance of harder/higher pH), dim/low lighting preference, no CO2 required, slow growth, midground/background placement, and propagation via division plus leaf-borne plantlets.

additional.name: AquaMate parent entry: Java Fern (fw-plant-019)
additional.notes: Inherited the broader water-parameter tolerance ranges (pH 5.5–7.5, gH 2–15, kH 0–14), epiphyte/rhizome care rules, family/order (Polypodiaceae/Polypodiales), and Southeast Asian native-range data from the parent species entry. 'Narrow' is a cultivar of Microsorum pteropus, so parent-species values apply except where Narrow-specific sources differ.
